result.summary: memcache.googleapis.com exposes a Google Cloud Memorystore for Memcached management REST API, and Google Cloud CLI commands (`gcloud memcache`) that operate against it; both use Google Cloud OAuth/Application Default Credentials.
result.description: Google Cloud Memorystore for Memcached is Google Cloud’s managed Memcached service for creating and operating Memcached instances. The service is used to provision, configure, and manage cache instances within Google Cloud projects.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud access token / application default credentials
gcp_oauth.setup: For user-driven CLI access, install the Google Cloud CLI and run `gcloud auth login` or `gcloud auth application-default login`. For service-to-service access, create a service account in the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/), grant it Memorystore IAM permissions, and obtain Application Default Credentials as described in Google Cloud auth setup. The Memcache API uses Google Cloud IAM, so the caller needs a Google-issued OAuth 2.0 access token tied to a user or service account with the right roles.

surfaces.name: Google Cloud Memorystore for Memcached API
